summary: EXP-408 closes ISS-1521 (HOL-CROSSREF.c2 finding #3, severity=moderate, 21 imprecise part-level hrefs). 18 surgical sections.json patches replace href="#partN" with subsection-precise anchors so the delegated click handler in docs/index.html scrolls readers to the named subsection rather than the top of the tab. The fixed instances span part1 (3), part4 (1), part5 (2), part6 (6), part7 (2), part8 (2). Two "Section 2.1" instances (part2b, part6) are deliberately deferred — no #section-2-1 / #p2-21 anchor exists in part2.html, so the part-level fallback is the best available target until a 2.1 anchor is added (filed as follow-up in EXP-408.follow_ups). One instance has been fixed since the c2 audit (curmudgeon counted 21, current state shows 20). BONUS: the part7/Section 7.2 patch additionally fixes a latent inline-onclick tab-routing bug — showTab("selftest") was being called for a Section 7.2 link, but part7 belongs to the falsify tab; corrected inline since the patch already rewrites that <a> tag. Pre-flight: every current_text locator is unique within its target section.html (verified via indexOf + nextIdx scan and sequential-apply simulation). Each new anchor id verified to exist exactly once in its containing part. review_class=verification (PROP-025) — batchable with adjacent verification items per curmudgeon Step 8a. Recommended decider action: integrate all 18 patches into data/sections.json, rebuild HTML, run tests, close ISS-1521.
